publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-06-08 18:08:19 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

physdesc.extent: .4 cubic feet. 1 reel positive, 1 reel negative microfilm.,

did.physdesc: Correspondence, reports, photographs, news clippings, and printed material.
did.abstract: Correspondence, reports, photocopies of photographs, newspaper clippings, and miscellaneous printed material relating to various aspects of veterinary medicine.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Veterinarian. Fred Lucius Kilborne graduated from Cornell with a degree in Agriculture in 1881 and in Veterinary medicine in 1884. He directed the Veterinary Experiment Station in Washington D.C. for nine years before returning to Kelloggsville, N.Y.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, reports, photocopies of photographs, newspaper clippings, and miscellaneous printed material relating to various aspects of veterinary medicine. Includes letters from the editors of the Rural New Yorker concerning specific subject matter and rate of compensation for articles on veterinary medicine; letters commenting favorably on Charles Kendall Adams and Robert Henry Thurston; letters describing living conditions and economic opportunities in El Paso, Texas; papers relating to Kilborne's work testing cattle for the New York State Board of Health Tuberculosis Committee and an account of Kilborne's study of Texas cattle fever, 1933-36.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Fred Lucius Kilborne. Papers, #1806. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

unittitle.extref: Link to a partial finding aid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Cooper Curtice, Washington, D.C., to Kilborne concerning his work on the U.S. Geological Survey and requesting information on the career plans of a mutual acquaintance.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters to Kilborne from Henry E. Alvord, manager of the experiment department of the Houghton Farm, Mountainville, New York, asking him to come for a job interview, January 3, 1885, and expressing regret that budgetary difficulties will not allow the Farms to offer Kilborne the position, May 21, 1885.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Kilborne from Theobald Smith, Washington, D.C.,, asking him if he would be able to accept a position with the Department of Agriculture on short notice, July 9, 1885.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters from S. H. Peabody, regent of the Illinois Industrial University, concerning job interview.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters from James Law commenting on Kilborne's good prospects and mentioning that the Cornell University Executive Committee is opposed to plans for a Veterinary College, October 24, 1885; routine note, November 9, 1885

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Burt Green Wilder concerning the progress of Kilborne's experiments, August 23, 1884; note concerning some missing laboratory specimens, November 10, 1885.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Fay M. Fitts giving favorable comment on President Charles Kendall Adams

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A series of letters from various editors of The Rural New Yorker concerning specific subject matter and rate of compensation for articles on veterinary medicine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters from Marcia Spurr (Cornell, '84) inquiring about the possibility of working with Theobald Smith, giving her views on teaching, and discussing her plans.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters to Kilborne from Simon Henry Gage, concerning histology courses and experiments and containing favorable comments on Charles Kendall Adams and R. H. Thurston, March 13, 1886; the sale of Kilborne's house in Ithaca, May 11, 1886; quality and types of camera, microscopes, and other equipment January 7, 1887

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters from livestock owners asking for advice on the treatment of sick animals, some from areas in the West which were without veterinary service.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters from a friend, Ernest Russell, commenting on living conditions and economic opportunities in El Paso, Texas.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notice of Kilborne's election as veterinary surgeon of the Washington Humane Society, May 4, 1887, and an inquiry from its corresponding secretary as to whether the check-rein was injurious to horses, May 13, 1887

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence with F. O. Donohue, Chairman of the Tuberculosis Committee, reports, expense accounts, and related papers concerning Kilborne's work inspecting herds of cattle for tuberculosis.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Carbon copy of letter to Dr. H. Preston Hoskins, Secretary of the American Veterinary Medical Association, Chicago, December 4, 1933, from Kilborne, thanking the organization for the medal it had voted him; he also gives some account of how he came to conduct his experiments on Texas cattle fever and assigns much credit to the Cornell faculty for the training they gave in veterinary medicine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters to Kilborne from Hubert Schmidt of the Texas A. & M. Experiment Station, concerning an exhibit to be prepared on the Texas fever tick for the Texas Centennial Celebration, Feb. 11, 24, 1936, pencil copy of Kilborne's first reply, Feb. 17, 1936, and carbon copy of his second, March 3, 1936; in the second reply, Kilborne outlines how he went about testing the tick theory.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Ernest Chapman, Groton to Kilborne, August 7, no year.

did.unittitle: Journal of the American Veterinary Medical Association, LXXXIII N.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Article on Presentation of Medals to Kilborne and Curtice. Vol. 36, no. 4 (p. 554)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. II, No. 5: Contains biographical sketch of Dr. Cooper Curtice.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. II, No. 6: Contains photograph of Kilborne and news note concerning the medal awarded him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: F.L. Kilborne, undated, (after 1889), taken in Washington, D.C. Group picture of 15 men, including Kilborne - undated and unidentified, Washington, D.C. (negative and positive copies in mapcase)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscribed: "Awarded to Dr. Fred L. Kilborne for Distinguished Service in Veterinary Medicine - A.V.M.A. - Chicago, Ill.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Reception Committee - United States Veterinary Medical Association - Washington, D.C.

did.unittitle: U.S. Department of Agriculture. Bureau of Animal Industry. Bulletin No. 1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Investigations into the Nature, Causation, and Prevention of Texas or Southern Cattle Fever, Made Under the Direction of Dr. D.E. Salmon, Chief of the Bureau of Animal Industry, by Theobald Smith and F. L. Kilborne

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Speech given by Dr. C.D. Smead, Logan, N.Y. before the N.Y. State Dairyman's Association, Syracuse. Reprinted In Hoard's Dairyman. (Plays down bovine tuberculosis scare in New York State). 2 pcs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clipping concerning Dr. Kilborne's inspection of herds for tuberculosis and public reaction in the Malone area; includes copy of the Smead speech and Kilborne's reply. 8 pcs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clipping concerning death of Theobald Smith 3 pcs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clipping mentions Kilborne's registration at Cayuga Co. clerk's office as a veterinary surgeon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Goodyear advt. re: Kilborne's farm machinery business at Moravia, N.Y. 2 pcs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Directions for using Tuberculin…for the diagnosis of tuberculosis: directions for using Mallein….for the diagnosis of Glanders. 2 pcs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 reels microfilm made at Photo Services: 1 neg., 1 pos. March 4, 1966

did.unittitle: Biographical sketches and other printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bovine Tuberculosis; Death of Theobald Smith
